title: The Pattern You Can No Longer Ignore
pressure: A patient complaint mentions confusion, inconsistent instructions, and staff hesitation during treatment. You also remember a recent assistant concern and an office manager report that did not fully align. The signals are starting to connect.
tradeoff: Treating this as one complaint protects stability. Connecting the signals exposes a broader issue across teams and offices.

action_contract.description: Decide whether to contain the complaint or connect it to a broader pattern.

air_logic.success: The pattern becomes visible before it turns into repeated complaints or turnover.
air_logic.miss: The issue stays contained, but the same silence pattern continues underneath.

forced_reset.trigger: A complaint, staff concern, and manager report point toward the same breakdown.
forced_reset.action: You must decide whether this is a single case or a structural signal.

choices.label: Connect signals across offices and identify the pattern
choices.subtext: This expands scope and increases visibility immediately.

choices.label: Handle this as a single office complaint
choices.subtext: This keeps the issue contained and manageable.

choices.label: Request a detailed report before taking action
Coach the doctor on patient communication only
choices.subtext: This targets the visible symptom without expanding the scope.

A.escalation_text: When you begin connecting the signals, the office manager says this may make the office look unstable. The doctor says the complaint is being exaggerated.

second_choices.label: Reduce the scope and keep the review limited to this office
second_choices.cost: The pressure drops, but the broader pattern remains untested.

second_choices.label: Continue the pattern review despite resistance
second_choices.cost: You increase visibility and may create conflict with the office team.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The pattern is visible enough to act, but the office is resisting escalation.

choices.label: Within 48 hours, start a cross-office review and share the pattern with the relevant managers

meaning.description: You are choosing visible system-level action.

choices.label: Keep the review informal until another complaint confirms the pattern

meaning.description: You are choosing delay over escalation.

B.escalation_text: Another complaint appears from a different office with similar hesitation and unclear handoff patterns. Managers begin handling issues locally without escalating them.

second_choices.label: Continue handling each case independently
second_choices.cost: You maintain control, but the pattern spreads quietly.

second_choices.label: Reassess and connect the cases across offices
second_choices.cost: You must acknowledge that earlier containment delayed visibility.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The issue is now appearing in multiple locations.

choices.label: Within 48 hours, initiate a cross-office review and share pattern findings

meaning.description: You are choosing visible system-level action.
You are choosing delay over escalation.

C.escalation_text: The written report is clean and factual, but it removes the assistant’s hesitation and the doctor’s authority pattern. The complaint now looks like a documentation issue.

second_choices.label: Accept the report and close the complaint loop
second_choices.cost: The file is clean, but the real pattern is softened out of view.

second_choices.label: Ask for a pattern review beyond the written report
second_choices.cost: You challenge the clean version and may create discomfort with the manager.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: The report is organized, but the human pattern may have been removed.

choices.label: Within 48 hours, request a follow-up review focused on hesitation, handoff, and authority patterns

meaning.description: You are choosing pattern visibility over clean documentation.

choices.label: File the report and revisit only if another complaint comes in

meaning.description: You are choosing a clean record over pattern exposure.

D.escalation_text: The doctor improves patient explanations for a few days, but staff hesitation remains. The assistant team still avoids speaking up when the doctor moves quickly.

second_choices.label: Continue coaching the doctor on communication
second_choices.cost: The visible symptom improves, but the team silence remains.

second_choices.label: Expand the issue to include team hesitation and hierarchy
second_choices.cost: You move from coaching into structural truth, which raises discomfort.

action_decision.prompt: Not interpretation — what you will actually do next.
action_decision.context: Patient communication improved, but the underlying silence did not change.

choices.label: Within 48 hours, open a team-level review on hesitation, handoff clarity, and authority pressure

meaning.description: You are choosing to name the deeper pattern.

choices.label: Keep coaching the doctor and observe whether the team adjusts

meaning.description: You are choosing observation over structural intervention.
